claim: Differentiated E2f-1, -2, -3 TKO cells in the intestine can exhibit apoptosis.

result.claim: Differentiated E2f-1, -2, -3 TKO cells in the intestine can exhibit apoptosis.
result.reasoning: The searches for propositions and chunks directly related to "Differentiated E2f-1, -2, -3 TKO cells in the intestine can exhibit apoptosis" did not yield any specific evidence. The results were general propositions about apoptosis or related to other biological contexts, not the specific E2f TKO cells in the intestine. Therefore, there is insufficient evidence in the knowledge base to support or refute the claim.

evidence.text: Exogenous expression of GEF-H1 and ECT2 prevents endomitosis.
evidence.source: Role of RhoA-specific guanine exchange factors in regulation of endomitosis in megakaryocytes.

evidence.text: Mitochondria are involved in apoptosis.
evidence.source: Adenine Nucleotide Translocase, Mitochondrial Stress, and Degenerative Cell Death

evidence.text: Single cells experiencing lateral interface contractility are eliminated from tissues by apoptosis.
evidence.source: Interface Contractility between Differently Fated Cells Drives Cell Elimination and Cyst Formation

evidence.text: Signaling via the intrinsic mitochondrial pathway of apoptosis is a critical signal transduction cascade.
evidence.source: Shifting the balance of mitochondrial apoptosis: therapeutic perspectives

evidence.text: Both SkQT1 and SkQ1 promoted cell survival.
evidence.source: New Data on Effects of SkQ1 and SkQT1 on Rat Liver Mitochondria and Yeast Cells

evidence.text: Mitochondria participate in apoptosis.
evidence.source: Mitochondria: Dynamic Organelles in Disease, Aging, and Development

evidence.text: The intrinsic mitochondrial pathway of apoptosis is typically altered in human cancers.
evidence.source: Shifting the balance of mitochondrial apoptosis: therapeutic perspectives

evidence.text: The correlation between apoptosis and MPT64 expression was not significant in ECs.
evidence.source: Differential expression of mycobacterial antigen MPT64, apoptosis and inflammatory markers in multinucleated giant cells and epithelioid cells in granulomas caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis

evidence.text: The PI3K/Akt/mTOR pathway is involved in the regulation of mitochondria-mediated apoptosis.
evidence.source: Shifting the balance of mitochondrial apoptosis: therapeutic perspectives

evidence.text: There was a significant negative correlation between apoptosis and MPT64 expression in MGCs (r = −0.569, p = 0.003).
evidence.source: Differential expression of mycobacterial antigen MPT64, apoptosis and inflammatory markers in multinucleated giant cells and epithelioid cells in granulomas caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis

evidence.text: ARID1A mutation inactivates the apoptosis-promoting function of p53 by upregulating HDAC6.
evidence.source: ARID1A-mutated ovarian cancers depend on HDAC6 activity

evidence.text: Pharmacological inhibition of mitogen-activated protein kinase kinase (MEK) activates apoptosis but not cell-cycle arrest in the inducible mouse model.
evidence.source: Oncogenic NRAS signaling differentially regulates survival and proliferation in melanoma

evidence.text: Activation-induced cell death was increased in human CD3 gamma-deficient T cell lines.
evidence.source: Contribution of CD3g to TCR regulation and signaling in human mature T lymphocytes

evidence.text: The total number of apoptotic MGCs was higher than the number of apoptotic ECs (p = 0.04).
evidence.source: Differential expression of mycobacterial antigen MPT64, apoptosis and inflammatory markers in multinucleated giant cells and epithelioid cells in granulomas caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis

evidence.text: The release of pro-apoptotic proteins occurs from the mitochondria.
evidence.source: Mitochondrial targeting of tBid/Bax: a role for the TOM complex?

evidence.text: There was a significant negative correlation between apoptosis and MPT64 expression in MGCs (r = −0.569, p = 0.003).
evidence.source: Differential expression of mycobacterial antigen MPT64, apoptosis and inflammatory markers in multinucleated giant cells and epithelioid cells in granulomas caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis

evidence.text: The expression of HEL-1 in the intestine and neurons contributes to longevity.
evidence.source: RNA helicase HEL-1 promotes longevity by specifically activating DAF-16/FOXO transcription factor signaling inCaenorhabditis elegans

evidence.text: DUSP4 overexpression increased chemotherapy-induced apoptosis.
evidence.source: Profiling of residual breast cancers after neoadjuvant chemotherapy identifies DUSP4 deficiency as a mechanism of drug resistance

evidence.text: Transfer of CD4(+)CD45RB(hi)Nlrp12(-/-) T cells into immunodeficient mice led to more severe colitis.
evidence.source: The NLRP12 Sensor Negatively Regulates Autoinflammatory Disease by Modulating Interleukin-4 Production in T Cells.

evidence.text: BCL-2 is an antiapoptotic protein.
evidence.source: Antiapoptotic BCL-2 is required for maintenance of a model leukemia.

evidence.text: HDAC6 directly deacetylates Lys120 of p53, a pro-apoptotic post-translational modification.
evidence.source: ARID1A-mutated ovarian cancers depend on HDAC6 activity

evidence.text: The intrinsic apoptotic signalling pathway is the conduit by which at least one class of mtROS exercise their actions in the nematode Caenorhabditis elegans.
evidence.source: Mitochondrial ROS and the Effectors of the Intrinsic Apoptotic Pathway in Aging Cells: The Discerning Killers!

evidence.text: Members of the Bcl-2 family of proteins are involved in the regulation of mitochondria-mediated apoptosis.
evidence.source: Shifting the balance of mitochondrial apoptosis: therapeutic perspectives
